business.headline: Sebi proposes new salary disclosure rules for asset managersIn a consultation paper released on Wednesday, Sebi suggested that AMCs should be allowed to disclose the combined remuneration paid to executive-level employees along with the total number of such employees.

Markets end marginally higher as FMCG, banks gains cushion profit bookingThe BSE Sensex rose 64.42 points, or 0.09%, to close at 73,983.18. However, the NSE Nifty50 slipped 27.15 points, or 0.12%, to settle at 23,214.95.

EU widens Russia sanctions net, India-based entities among 50 firms targetedThe European Commission has proposed a new Russia sanctions package that could restrict India-based companies. The move widens scrutiny of third-country trade networks, but it still needs unanimous EU approval.

Zepto moves closer to IPO launch: What investors should watchAccording to the updated filing, Zepto's IPO will consist of a fresh issue of shares worth Rs 8,010 crore and an offer-for-sale (OFS) of 113 million shares by existing investors.

No 8th Pay Commission yet, but a DA hike could be around the cornerFresh inflation data has raised the possibility of a Dearness Allowance (DA) increase from July 2026 for central government employees and pensioners. The likely move could offer interim relief while the 8th Pay Commission continues to examine wider salary and pension demands.

Do you own too many mutual funds? How overdiversification can hurt returnsAdding a new mutual fund every year feels like a smart move. But at what point does diversification turn into overdiversification and start hurting returns? Let's find out.
Do stock market returns really beat FDs after taxes and risk?Ace investor Shankar Sharma has questioned whether stock markets really outperform fixed deposits once taxes and volatility are counted.

Will AI reduce hiring? TCS Chairman explains what lies ahead for IT jobsSpeaking at TCS's 31st annual general meeting, N Chandrasekaran outlined a future where AI agents become an integral part of the workforce.
Equity mutual fund inflows drop 40.4% month-on-month amid global uncertaintyAMFI data showed equity mutual fund inflows fell 40.4% to Rs 22,907.77 crore in May from Rs 38,440.20 crore in April.

Gold falls below Rs 1.5 lakh, silver drops Rs 4,000. Here's whyGold and silver slumped in India after heavy selling in global bullion markets. A stronger dollar, rising oil prices and revived rate hike fears deepened pressure on precious metals.

CleanMax shares jump 15% to record high: Why the stock is rising todayCleanMax and Meta have partnered to develop more than 900 MW of renewable energy capacity in India. The agreement has lifted investor interest as rising data centre and AI demand boosts the outlook for clean power.

Sensex up 500 points: Why are markets rising despite fresh US-Iran escalationSensex rose 500 points, while Nifty also gained. The positive move surprised many investors because geopolitical tensions usually trigger a selloff in risk assets.

Markets open higher as investors shrug off US-Iran conflict; RIL, HUL jump 2%The S&P BSE Sensex rose 337.02 points, or 0.46%, to 74,255.78 in early trade, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 80.35 points, or 0.35%, to trade at 23,322.45 as of 9:27 am.

Meta partners with Reliance for it's first AI data centre in IndiaMeta has partnered with Reliance Industries to lease a 168 MW AI-enabled data centre in Jamnagar. The deal expands their long-running relationship as both companies deepen their AI push in India.

How Trump's crypto empire made $2.3 billion while millions of investors lost bigUS President Donald Trump-linked crypto ventures generated about $2.3 billion for his family. It said more than a million investors lost roughly as much, underscoring the gap between promoter gains and buyer risk.

Standard deduction, NPS and more: 5 tax benefits still available under new regimeThe new tax regime doesn't necessarily close every tax-saving opportunity. Although most popular deductions are no longer available, a few vital benefits are still in place that can help lower your taxable income.

Finance Ministry holds borrowing plans steady, to reassess spending in OctoberSources said the Budget 2026-27 already factored in global uncertainties, giving the government room to handle external shocks.

Sensex, Nifty close in green as easing Israel-Iran tensions lift sentimentThe BSE Sensex gained 394.50 points, or 0.54%, to close at 73,918.76. The NSE Nifty50 rose 119.10 points, or 0.52%, to settle at 23,242.10.

The inspection that hid more than it revealed, and the enforcement reform now neededIn the 10th part of this series on labour laws and Inspector Raj, this article examines a case where detailed scrutiny of company records exposed inspection failures, missing disclosures and substantial under-reporting of coverable employees.

From Oman to Gujarat: The mega pipeline that could reshape India's energy futureEvery flare-up in the Strait of Hormuz sends shockwaves through global energy markets. Could a proposed 2,000-km undersea pipeline from Oman to Gujarat help India break free from that vulnerability? Let's find out.

entertainment.headline: Pakistani fan travels 20 km to meet Imtiaz Ali, AR Rahman at border, shares note A Pakistani fan travelled 20 km to the Attari-Wagah Border to meet Imtiaz Ali and AR Rahman during promotions for Main Vaapas Aaunga. He later shared a heartfelt note on social media, recalling the emotional cross-border encounter.
Bharathiraja to be cremated with full state honours in his hometown on June 11 Filmmaker Bharathiraja will be cremated with full state honours at his farmhouse in Theni district on June 11.

NBK111 glimpse: Nandamuri Balakrishna unveils new avatar on 65th birthday The makers of NBK111 released a special glimpse titled Entry of an Era on Nandamuri Balakrishna's 65th birthday, offering fans their first look at the actor's new avatar.

Kichcha Sudeep's nephew Sanchith on nepotism: My uncle can't act on my behalf Mango Pachcha actor Sanchith Sanjeev opened up about carrying Kichcha Sudeep's legacy, navigating nepotism, and why success ultimately depends on winning over audiences.

Is Alia's Alpha a copy of French thriller La Femme Nikita? Fans spot similarity Alpha's teaser has triggered online comparisons with cult films La Femme Nikita and American Sniper. The reaction has added a plagiarism debate to the buzz around YRF's female-led spy thriller.

Ahead of Welcome 3, Raveena Tandon shares throwback Mohra pic with Akshay, Suniel Raveena Tandon shared a series of BTS pictures from Welcome To The Jungle, but it was a throwback photo from the sets of Mohra featuring Akshay Kumar and Suniel Shetty that stole the spotlight.

Khushboo Patani slams misogynistic comments amid Pranit More row: Educated fools Khushboo Patani criticised comedian Pranit More after an audience member's 370 biryani remark about expecting a return drew outrage. The backlash widened as both men apologised and the audience member's employer ended his employment.

Sutapa Sikdar rejects Pranit More apology: Why should women always forgive? Sutapa Sikdar has rejected comedian Pranit More's apology over a viral crowdwork clip. Her response, posted with rape case data, challenged the idea that women must keep forgiving such behaviour.
Alpha's story credit goes to Uday Chopra, and the internet is stunned The teaser of Alpha has sparked discussion online after viewers noticed that the film's story has been credited to Uday Chopra.

Madhuri Dixit's OTT game is clear: The Dhak Dhak girl enters her intense era From the beloved 'Dhak Dhak' girl to a performer unafraid of morally complex and unconventional characters, Madhuri Dixit has reinvented herself on OTT. Her recent projects showcase a darker, bolder and more versatile side that goes far beyond the glamorous image that made her a Bollywood icon.

Malti Chahar slams Pranit More's Rs 370 biryani comedy, draws parallel with Peddi Malti Chahar has reacted strongly to the Rs 370 biryani remark made during Pranit More's show, linking it to wider issues of consent, objectification and women's independence.

I like you, not your acting: Rajinikanth recalls Bharathiraja's honesty in tribute Rajinikanth paid homage to Bharathiraja in Chennai and recalled the director's unfiltered honesty. He said the filmmaker's work, friendships and support for others would be remembered for years.

Bharathiraja dies at 84: Mohanlal, Rajinikanth, Allu Arjun and others pay tribute Veteran filmmaker Bharathiraja died in Chennai at 84 after a prolonged illness. Tributes from across South Indian cinema underlined his enduring influence on generations of artists.

Nobody minds SRK's abs, but outrage over Janhvi's scenes is justified. Here's why The backlash to Peddi has renewed scrutiny of how Hindi cinema frames women's bodies on screen. The debate has also highlighted how male sexualisation is treated as fleeting fun while objectification of women remains embedded in storytelling.

Breaking hearts to breaking bones: SRK smitten by Alia Bhatt in Alpha teaser Shah Rukh Khan, Amitabh Bachchan and others praised Alia Bhatt after the Alpha teaser introduced her as Sita, doing all-and-out action.

Bharathiraja: The man who made Kamal look 'ugly', cut a Rs 3,000 deal with Rajini Bharathiraja, the filmmaker who pitched 16 Vayathinile in a soiled dhoti and reshaped Tamil cinema, has died at 84 on June 10. His rural realism, outdoor locations and eye for Kamal Haasan, Rajinikanth and Sridevi altered Indian cinema's course.

Pranit More deactivates Instagram amid backlash over viral Rs 370 biryani clip Comedian Pranit More appears to have deactivated his Instagram account amid the backlash surrounding his viral 'Rs 370 ki biryani' crowdwork clip. The debate around the ongoing incident continues to intensify online.

Before defending Gurugram man in Pranit More's show, watch the entire video A Gurugram audience member at Pranit More's show recounted pursuing a woman after buying her biryani and described how he violated her. But really, have you watched the entire video from the show?

Dhamaal 4 gets a new release date as Ajay Devgn shares major update Ajay Devgn has shared a fresh update on Dhamaal 4, confirming its theatrical release date and unveiling a new look from the film. Here's what the latest announcement reveals.

general news.headline: US military hits targets in Iran, explosions heard in multiple regionsExplosions reported in Sirik and Minab as air defence activated in Iran after US strikes

India summons US diplomat over tanker strike off Oman; 3 Indians still missingThe MEA said 21 Indian sailors were rescued following the attack, while three remain missing. The US military subsequently admitted targeting the vessel during enforcement of its blockade on Iran.

Yusuf Pathan, Saayoni Ghosh, Shatrughan Sinha among 19 TMC rebel list signatoriesNineteen TMC MPs, including Yusuf Pathan, Saayoni Ghosh and Shatrughan Sinha allegedly joined the rebel camp backing the NDA, pushing the party further towards a possible vertical split.

Mumbai's new Rs 248-crore flyover looks like it has survived an earthquakeMumbai's Mrinaltai Gore flyover extension is like a metaphor for the city's collapsing infrastructure promises. It took eight years and Rs 248 crore to build this wonder, and after multiple cost escalations and vigilance warnings, the ribbon-cutting shows one of BMC's most iconic works, one where tyres are already sinking into the road.

Is Mamata merging TMC with Congress?There's an intense buzz that Mamata Banerjee, who has effectively lost control of her Trinamool Congress to rebels, might merge the party with the Congress. Her prolonged stay in New Delhi, her nephew Abhishek Banerje's meeting with Rahul Gandhi, and her meeting with Sonia Gandhi, are all unusual.

Strong, wise man: Trump congratulates PM Modi on completing 12 years in officePM Modi achieved the record on June 10, completing 4,399 consecutive days in office since first taking oath as prime minister on May 26, 2014.

China's dig at India over NEET row? Embassy flaunts glitch-free Gaokao examChina appeared to take a dig at India's competitive exam management, highlighting the glitch-free conduct of the Gaokao for nearly 1.3 crore students amid a huge controversy over the NEET-UG paper leak.

Live: US preparing major strikes on Iran tonight, Hegseth saysUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: Iran and the US have exchanged fresh military actions, marking another escalation in tensions around the Strait of Hormuz. Iran's IRGC claimed it launched Shahed-136 drones at the US Navy's Fifth Fleet headquarters in Bahrain and targeted a US-linked airbase in Jordan in retaliation for recent American strikes in southern Iran. Regional militaries responded by activating air defences, with Jordan reporting the interception of five Iranian missiles and Bahrain sounding air raid sirens. Kuwait also said it was tracking and intercepting hostile aerial threats. Earlier, Iran accused US forces of attacking several sites in the country's south, including the Sirik region. The US military described its strikes on Iranian air-defence and radar infrastructure as a proportional act of self-defence following attacks on US assets, including the reported downing of an Apache helicopter. The latest exchanges highlight the risk of broader regional instability despite ongoing diplomatic efforts. Follow IndiaToday.in for the latest updates.

Video: Auto driver brutally beats up elderly after he asks him not to spit on roadA shocking street confrontation caught on camera has snowballed into a major controversy, drawing intervention from Maharashtra's Deputy Chief Minister, a political manhunt and demands for the harshest possible charges.

I'm a career politician, not part-timer: Sushmita Dev defends TMC exit amid BJP buzzDefending her exit from the TMC and the Rajya Sabha, Sushmita Dev said she had taken a practical decision based on Assam's political realities, fuelling speculation that she could join the BJP as the Mamata Banerjee-led party grapples with an internal revolt.

E20, E30 fuel uncertainty could delay vehicle purchases for 43% of buyers: SurveyUncertainty surrounding India's ethanol-blending roadmap is beginning to influence vehicle-buying decisions, with a new survey revealing that concerns over E20 and potential E30 fuel adoption are causing hesitation among buyers.

Fresh Nipah case surfaces in Kerala, 40-year-old man tests positive in KozhikodeA fresh Nipah virus case was reported in Kerala after a 40-year-old man tested positive in a preliminary test in Kozhikode. Kerala has seen four Nipah outbreaks, with 31 cases and 24 deaths reported between 2018 and 2023.

The Supreme Court has reset the moral compass on pre-marital sexThe Supreme Court said a consensual relationship between unmarried adults cannot by itself define character. The observation shifts the conversation from moral judgment to consent, autonomy and personal choice.

1.5 lakh Kashmiris march across POK as Pakistan steps up crackdown on protestersPOK remained tense as over 1.5 lakh people joined anti-government protests days after a brutal crackdown killed 11 civilians. As demonstrations gathered steam, Pakistani authorities filed sedition cases against protest leaders, announced a Rs 1 crore bounty on four organisers and blocked key routes to Muzaffarabad.

Making sure right people come to US: Trump on visas for FIFA World CupUS President Donald Trump said his administration is working to ensure that "the right people" are allowed into the country for the FIFA World Cup 2026 as the United States prepares to host the tournament.

Mentally ill teen escapes hospital, runs over Indian-origin Canadian copIndian-origin constable Tarun Bali, who served with the Ontario Provincial Police, was killed after being hit by a vehicle during an operation near Hearst in Canada. The accused is an 18-year-old who allegedly escaped from a psychiatric facility while undergoing assessment under the Mental Health Act.

Maa Behen's witch narrative has roots in real historyIn Maa Behen, Rekha is branded a witch after her husband's death and years of rumour. The trope echoes a long history of suspicion around women whose beauty or influence unsettled society.

North India to see thunderstorms, hail and dust Thursday, temperatures to dropA western disturbance is set to bring thunderstorms, hailstorms, dust storms and gusty winds to north India on June 11, with Delhi expecting cooler weather and evening rain. IMD also forecasts very heavy rainfall across the northeast and south as the monsoon advances.

What's the case against Meenakshi Natarajan and who is the complainant?Congress leader Meenakshi Natarajan's Rajya Sabha nomination was rejected after the BJP alleged she failed to disclose a Telangana court matter linked to a private complaint by a former Congress worker. While Congress said no criminal case exists against her, the complainant accused party leaders, including Natarajan, of ignoring her molestation allegations.

Before defending Gurugram man in Pranit More's show, watch the entire videoA Gurugram audience member at Pranit More's show recounted pursuing a woman after buying her biryani and described how he violated her. But really, have you watched the entire video from the show?

Mumbai's Mithi river lies choked ahead of monsoon; BMC misses cleaning deadlineDespite the June 10 deadline for the BMC's Mithi river desilting project, India Today's ground inspections found several stretches still clogged with vegetation, waste and silt, with no labourers or machinery on site.

Isro's LVM3 that launched Chandrayaan-3 to be handed to private sectorIN-SPACe has invited Indian companies to take over the end-to-end realisation, operation and commercialisation of Isro's LVM3 rocket.

Congress growth rate brought misery to India: PM in 12-year anniversary speechMarking 12 years of the NDA government, Prime Minister Narendra Modi said India's slow-growth era was wrongly branded the "Hindu growth rate" and should instead have been called the "Congress growth rate". He accused the party of pushing the country into "helplessness, misery and an inferiority complex".

Owaisi questions SIR form's 5 children limit: Even PM's family has 6 siblingsAIMIM chief Asaduddin Owaisi questioned why the SIR enumeration form allows details of only up to five children and argued that no law bars citizens with larger families from voting.

sports.headline: Ben Stokes made a mistake, but shouldn't be sacked as Test captain: Michael Vaughan Michael Vaughan has defended Ben Stokes amid scrutiny over his curfew breach, arguing that while the England captain made a mistake, the incident does not justify removing him from the Test captaincy.

Making sure right people come to US: Trump on visas for FIFA World Cup US President Donald Trump said his administration is working to ensure that "the right people" are allowed into the country for the FIFA World Cup 2026 as the United States prepares to host the tournament.

Hitman in range: Rohit Sharma gets into groove for India vs Afghanistan ODIs Rohit Sharma returned to India's white-ball nets and gradually found his range after a lay-off. The session eased immediate worries and showed a senior batter measuring himself against his own standards.

Explosive Richa Ghosh finds form, but India lose final T20 World Cup warm-up Women's T20 World Cup 2026: Richa Ghosh hit 68 off 36, but India lost to England in Cardiff in their final warm-up match. Her counterattack eased concerns before Sunday's World Cup opener, though India's top order still remains under scrutiny.

FIFA World Cup coverage in India headlined by Igor Stimac, Gurpreet Sandhu With the FIFA World Cup 2026 just days away, ZEE5 has assembled a star-studded multilingual commentary team featuring Bhaichung Bhutia, Gurpreet Singh Sandhu, Igor Stimac and I.M. Vijayan to bring the tournament closer to football fans across India.

After missing IPL, MS Dhoni promotes inaugural edition of Jharkhand T20 league MS Dhoni attended the opening ceremony of the inaugural Jharkhand T20 League in Ranchi on Wednesday. His presence gave the new tournament a high-profile start.

FIFA World Cup fever grips Kerala as God's own country prepares for tournament World Cup fever has engulfed Kerala, with flags, cutouts and flex battles stretching across the state. Beneath the carnival sits a familiar truth: deep football culture, thin grassroots support and one more emotional wait for Messi, Ronaldo and Neymar.

Ben Stokes, Gus Atkinson dropped from England Test squad after nightclub incident England captain Ben Stokes and Gus Atkinson have been dropped for the second Test against New Zealand after breaching a midnight curfew at a London nightclub. Stokes is reportedly considering his future as the Test captain of England.

Security breach sees Messi, Argentina's World Cup squad's passport details leaked An official team sheet at Alabama's Jordan-Hare Stadium reportedly exposed the passport details of Lionel Messi and Argentina's entire squad before the Iceland friendly. The leak raised fresh concerns over player privacy and data security days before Argentina begin their World Cup defence.

Manchester United captain Fernandes, Keane bury hatchet after candid chat Roy Keane said he has spoken to Bruno Fernandes after a row over comments about the Premier League assist record between the duo. The exchange closed a misunderstanding that Fernandes said had distorted his original remarks.

Haiti will wear Poland's flag on their FIFA World Cup 2026 jersey. Here's why Haiti are set to make their second appearance at the FIFA World Cup this year, but there seems to be a small tribute on the kit to Poland with the presence of their flag.

World Cup, Group J preview: Defenders Argentina prepare for Messi's farewellFIFA World Cup 2026: Lionel Messi's Argentina prepare for one last dance for the legendary footballer. Argentina have been drawn against Algeria, Austria and Jordan in the Group stage.

Australian Badminton: Sindhu storms into 2nd round, Tanvi upsets 5th seedIndia's PV Sindhu and emerging star Tanvi Sharma marched into the second round of the Australian Badminton Open. Sindhu, having returned to Top 10 in singles rankings hammered Peru's Ines Castillo in straight games.

Priyank Panchal launches India's first athlete-led sports education hubSportsversity has launched an athlete-first sports education platform with Priyank Panchal as strategic adviser. The venture aims to open structured pathways into non-playing careers across India's expanding sports industry.

Shubman Gill becomes top-ranked Indian Test batter, Jasprit Bumrah retains top spotIndia captain Shubman Gill is now the country's top Test batter in the latest ICC rankings, while Jasprit Bumrah remains the No.1 Test bowler. Harry Brook climbs to the global summit.

Pakistan cricket to decide futures of captain Masood, Sarfaraz over weekendPakistan Cricket Board officials will meet this weekend to decide whether Shan Masood and Sarfaraz stay in charge after the 0-2 defeat in Bangladesh. The review could trigger a wider reset, with Salman Ali Agha, Younis Khan and governance changes all in play.

Breakout Test spinner Manav Suthar signs County Championship contractIndia's latest Test sensation, Manav Suthar has bagged a stint in County Cricket. The off-spinner is set to play for Warwickshire for two matches this season.

Somalia gives hero's welcome to FIFA World Cup referee denied entry by US: VideoSomalia gave Omar Abdulkadir Artan a hero's welcome after US authorities denied him entry for the 2026 FIFA World Cup. The award-winning referee landed in the capital city of Mogadishu on Wednesday, June 10.

Curacao to Cape Verde: The unmissable minnows at FIFA World Cup 2026From Curacao's historic debut to long-awaited returns for Haiti and DR Congo, alongside Cape Verde's tactical brilliance, the expanded 48-team FIFA World Cup gives football's ultimate minnows a shot at global immortality. Here is all that you need to know about them.

Camera comes crashing down in football match, crew narrowly escapes huge mishapWhile the FIFA World Cup has been muddled with on and off-field controversies, things are not better for the federations outside of the global tournament as well. The latest disaster has taken place in a friendly between Hungary and Kazakhstan, where a spider-cam suspended with wires above the stadium, came crashing down on the pitch.

Asian Games: No Babar, Shaheen as Sahibzada Farhan to lead Pakistan's young squadPakistan have handed Sahibzada Farhan the captaincy of a youthful squad for the 2026 Asian Games, leaving out several established white-ball stars. The selection signals a shift in focus following recent tournament disappointments.

England can dare to dream but are not heavy favourites, says Thomas TuchelEngland manager Thomas Tuchel believes his side have the quality to challenge for the World Cup title, but insists they cannot be considered favourites given their long wait for success on football's biggest stage.

After Virat Kohli, Hardik Pandya ruled out of ODI series vs AfghanistanAfter Virat Kohli, Hardik Pandya has been ruled out of India's ruled out of the 3-match ODI series vs Afghanistan. BCCI sources told India Today that Hardik is suffering a quadricep injury which will take three weeks to heal.

Lionel Messi scores on comeback as Argentina cruise past Iceland 3-0Lionel Messi marked his return from injury with a goal as Argentina completed their FIFA World Cup preparations in style, beating Iceland 3-0 in a friendly at Auburn, Alabama, on Tuesday.

Writing exams and beating Full Members? Netherlands women eye more at T20 World CupThe Netherlands head into their maiden Women's T20 World Cup believing they can compete with the sport's biggest teams after a historic win over Bangladesh. For a squad balancing studies, jobs, and cricket, the tournament is also a chance to earn franchise opportunities and keep their cricketing dreams alive.

World Championship a burden? Most people lucky not to achieve it, says D GukeshBleeding rating points and fresh off a bruising Norway Chess campaign, World Champion D Gukesh opens up about the devastating psychological toll of climbing the peak too early.

Can India Women end T20 World Cup heartbreak after ODI glory?ICC Women's T20 World Cup: Harmanpreet Kaur guided India to their maiden ICC title last year. Now, the Indian skipper will be hoping to carry that winning momentum into the Women's T20 World Cup, a trophy that has so far eluded India despite several near misses on the global stage.

Inglis disappointed as Australia suffer first ODI loss to Bangladesh in 21 yearsBangladesh beat Australia by 86 runs in the first ODI in Dhaka, with Josh Inglis left frustrated by a flat team performance. The defeat ended Australia's 21-year unbeaten run against Bangladesh in ODIs and handed the hosts a 1-0 lead in the three-match series.

Atletico ridicule Real Madrid after rejecting blockbuster offer for Julian AlvarezReal Madrid's attempt to sign Julian Alvarez for a club-record €150 million was met with ridicule from Atletico Madrid, who swiftly rejected the offer and publicly taunted their city rivals.

Serena Williams mulls Wimbledon comeback after winning return to tennisSerena Williams has not ruled out a return to Wimbledon after making a winning comeback to professional tennis nearly four years after her last competitive match.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
